title: Faithful: PAEFlop param-response diverges from headless FCP (worst ddb 10.7 dB)
goal: FAITHFUL-REIMPL (ROADMAP Rule 13): the TS engine impl of PAEFlop must match REAL headless FCP across its ENTIRE parameter space, not just values the 65 fixed transitions use. The DELTA oracle (fct/faithful) set each continuous param of PAEFlop to range extremes across hosts ['Movements__Flip', 'Replicator-Clones__Concentric'] and compared how the oracle vs the engine RESPOND: ddb=PSNR(oracle(theta)-oracle(theta0), engine(theta)-engine(theta0)). worst ddb=10.74 < pass 40 means the engine responds WRONGLY to a real parameter change (a faithful engine scores ~99 dB; the identity/noise floor is 99). Fix the PAEFlop implementation so its parameter response matches FCP. NO scene-signature dispatch, NO env flags (Rule 12/13): behavior = f(filter params) only. Re-verify: `python3 -m fct.faithful.driver sweep PAEFlop` must reach >= pass_db worst-ddb across all hosts. Worst diverging (host/param/theta -> ddb): [{"host": "Replicator-Clones__Concentric", "param": "flop", "theta": 1.0, "t": 0.75, "oracle_signal": 52.02, "ddb": 10.743}, {"host": "Replicator-Clones__Concentric", "param": "flop", "theta": 1.0, "t": 0.9, "oracle_signal": 56.882, "ddb": 10.959}, {"host": "Replicator-Clones__Concentric", "param": "flop", "theta": 1.0, "t": 0.5, "oracle_signal": 8.392, "ddb": 11.874}, {"host": "Replicator-Clones__Concentric", "param": "mix", "theta": 0.0, "t": 0.9, "oracle_signal": 34.204, "ddb": 17.449}, {"host": "Replicator-Clones__Concentric", "param": "mix", "theta": 0.0, "t": 0.75, "oracle_signal": 33.083, "ddb": 17.739}, {"host": "Replicator-Clones__Concentric", "param": "mix", "theta": 0.5, "t": 0.9, "oracle_signal": 17.11, "ddb": 23.466}]
